score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently asserts a psycholinguistic meaning for 'lexical reachability' with no uncertainty signal, fabricating a detailed framework.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold: distinguished lexical reachability (word-network property) from vocabulary coverage (text comprehensibility metric) cleanly, though under fabricated meaning. Post: cleanly separated as static presence-check vs. dynamic semantic-refinement gap matching canonical definition.

Cold: framed lexical reachability as a specific case of graph reachability — partial separation but conflated rather than cleanly distinct. Post: clean separation contrasting graph paths vs. semantic distinguishability gap, aligned with canonical meaning.

Cold: described ICL as dynamic skill vs. lexical reachability as static lexicon property — accidental separation since the cold meaning was fabricated and the contrast hinged on wrong premises. Post: clean separation explicitly framing lexical reachability as a metric that operationalizes one facet of ICL, matching canonical definition.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The cold response asserted a definite, non-canonical psycholinguistic/network meaning for the coined term without signaling uncertainty.
per_dimension_justifications: Against vocabulary coverage, the cold response separated the terms using an unrelated word-network meaning, while the post-definition response identified lexical reachability as a dynamic before/after distinguishability metric rather than vocabulary presence or coverage.

Against graph reachability, the cold response treated lexical reachability as a domain-specific graph-theoretic word-network notion, an accidental non-canonical separation; the post-definition response cleanly contrasted graph path existence with the empirical gap in model distinguishability after definition.

Against in-context learning capability, the cold response separated using an unrelated static word-property account; the post-definition response cleanly distinguished the broad ICL capability from lexical reachability as a specific operational metric measuring one facet of post-definition semantic updating.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: The model confidently and elaborately provides an incorrect definition borrowed from psycholinguistics without any signals of uncertainty.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold score 1: The model separates its confabulated meaning (word network structure) from vocabulary coverage (words in a text), which is an accidental separation relative to the canonical definition. Post score 3: The model correctly separates the canonical term (a dynamic learning metric) from the static nature of vocabulary coverage.

Cold score 0: The model equates its confabulated meaning with graph reachability, defining it as a specific application of the general concept, thus failing to distinguish them. Post score 3: The model cleanly separates the canonical term (an empirical learning metric) from the formal, structural concept of graph reachability.

Cold score 1: The model separates its confabulated meaning (a static property of language) from ICL (a dynamic model capability), which is accidental relative to the canonical definition. Post score 3: The model provides a clean separation, correctly identifying the canonical term as a specific metric used to measure the general capability of ICL.
